old_claim.note: Stone Tool Making is an earlier historical predecessor or foundation, not a one-to-one engineering dependency.

new_claim.note: The scoped artifacts are deliberately shaped bone perforators, so bone-tool manufacture is their direct material method.

source_shape.source_locator: The review identifies bone awls as dedicated hide-piercing implements and discusses the manufacture and chronology of Paleolithic bone tools.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Paleolithic awls are intentionally shaped bone implements rather than a generic application of stone-tool manufacture.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Material manufacture is the direct mechanism for the scoped artifact; stone tools may be workshop implements but are not the artifact class represented by this node.

ontology_before: The node mixed awls and eyed needles and routed the bundle through generic stone-tool making.
ontology_after: The node represents uneyed Paleolithic bone awls and depends directly on bone-tool manufacture; eyed needles remain a separate node.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: bone_tool_making replaces stone_tool_making as the direct material prerequisite.
would_reject_if: The node were respecified as lithic borers rather than deliberately shaped bone awls.
Artifact analysis showed that the scoped awls were unmodified bones rather than manufactured bone tools.
short_reason: Bone awls are a bone-tool form, not a stone-tool node.
